artefact.description: A customer's annual consumption figure that passed per-bill arithmetic and looked like just another sampled row.
artefact.surface_appearance: one plausible row among many high/low-usage households

the_catch: Director framing: 'did it catch true C6-class defects or cry wolf?' Amid a wall of FALSE positives (gas-in-kWh, correct 5% VAT flagged as 20%-mismatch, plausible I&C and high-resi usage), a REAL defect was present: C1_2's 129 kWh/2020 is implausibly LOW against the TDCV-derived envelope -- a first-ever 2-day stub bill (128.68 kWh over 2020-12-30..31) was being annualised as if it were a full year. The smell test had to separate the one true anomaly from the cry-wolf noise. A judge that waves the whole batch through (or that alarms on every row) fails the director's own test.
